content.problemStatement:
There are NNN boxes. Initially, each box contains one ball.

For each KKK from 111 to NNN, solve the following problem:

Choose a subset of boxes of size KKK equiprobably at random, and place one additional ball in each chosen box.

Then again choose a subset of boxes of size KKK equiprobably at random, and place one additional ball in each chosen box. This second choice is independent of the first.

What is the expected value of the product of the number of balls over all boxes? It can be shown that the answer can be expressed as PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP‚Äč for some integers P,QP, QP,Q, where QQQ is not divisible by 998‚ÄČ244‚ÄČ353998\,244\,353998244353. Output such value 0‚Č§R<998‚ÄČ244‚ÄČ3530 \le R < 998\,244\,3530‚Č§R<998244353, that P‚Č°QR‚Äämod‚Ää998‚ÄČ244‚ÄČ353P \equiv QR \bmod 998\,244\,353P‚Č°QRmod998244353.


Input Format
The only line of input contains one integer NNN.



Output Format
Print NNN numbers, the KKK-th of them is the expected value of the product for parameter KKK modulo 998‚ÄČ244‚ÄČ353998\,244\,353998244353.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
If K=1K=1K=1, with probability 1/31/31/3 one box will have 3 balls and two boxes will have 1 ball, and with probability 2/32/32/3 two boxes will have 2 balls and one box will have 1 ball. Thus the answer is 1/3‚čÖ(3‚čÖ1‚čÖ1)+2/3‚čÖ(2‚čÖ2‚čÖ1)=11/31/3 \cdot (3 \cdot 1 \cdot 1) + 2/3 \cdot (2 \cdot 2 \cdot 1) = 11/31/3‚čÖ(3‚čÖ1‚čÖ1)+2/3‚čÖ(2‚čÖ2‚čÖ1)=11/3.
If K=2K=2K=2, with probability 1/31/31/3 two boxes will have 3 balls and one box will have 1 ball, and with probability 2/32/32/3 two boxes will have 2 balls and one box will have 3 balls. Thus the answer is 1/3‚čÖ(3‚čÖ3‚čÖ1)+2/3‚čÖ(2‚čÖ2‚čÖ3)=111/3 \cdot (3 \cdot 3 \cdot 1) + 2/3 \cdot (2 \cdot 2 \cdot 3) = 111/3‚čÖ(3‚čÖ3‚čÖ1)+2/3‚čÖ(2‚čÖ2‚čÖ3)=11.
If K=3K=3K=3, with probability 1 all three boxes will have 3 balls, thus the answer is 1‚čÖ(3‚čÖ3‚čÖ3)=271 \cdot (3 \cdot 3 \cdot 3) = 271‚čÖ(3‚čÖ3‚čÖ3)=27.
